page: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-48 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the n4a server family. It features 48 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 CPU architecture, paired with 96.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, prioritizing raw processing power over memory capacity.

This server configuration does not include local storage, requiring reliance on network-attached storage volumes for persistent data. It features zero GPUs and does not come with complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage and hardware accelerators represents a design tradeoff, optimizing the instance for pure CPU-bound workloads rather than data-heavy storage operations or parallel graphics processing.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the n4a-highcpu-48 offers a high density of dedicated compute resources relative to its memory footprint. This makes it a viable option for workloads that demand significant CPU capacity without the overhead of excess RAM. Suitable workloads include batch processing, CI/CD compilation pipelines, web serving, and distributed compute tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4a-highcpu-48 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server from the n4a family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 96.0 GB of RAM. With a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU, this instance is designed for CPU-heavy workloads that do not require massive memory allocations. The server does not include local storage or GPUs, and it provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. This hardware profile makes the instance highly efficient for processing-intensive tasks where memory and local storage overhead can be minimized. It is best suited for workloads such as batch processing, web serving, and CI/CD pipelines.
